title: SSC CGL 2026 Sectional Timing Tier I 23 23

questions.question: A geometric progression (GP) has first term 4 and common ratio 2. Find the 4th term.
questions.explanation: The first term is 4 and the common ratio is 2 nth term = 𝑎 𝑟(n-1) 4th term = 𝑎 𝑟3 = 4 23 = 4 8 = 32

questions.question: If the given symbols mean: # means divided by @ means added to % means multiplied by & means subtracted from Find the value of the expression: 18 % 2 @ 6 # 3 & 4 = ?
questions.explanation: Replace the symbols with their actual meanings. % → multiplied by @ → added to # → divided by & → subtracted by So the expression becomes: 18 2 + 6 3 − 4 = 36 + 6 3 − 4 = 36 + 2 − 4 = 38 − 4 = 34

questions.question: Find the missing term (?) in the following series. 100, 198, 393, 781, ?, 3099
What value of ? makes (240 ÷ ( ?/4)) = 40
Robin's brother Samir is married to Neha, who is the only child of Riya. Riya's daughter-in-law Karuna is married to Tarun. How is Tarun related to Robin?
questions.explanation: Robin's brother Samir is married to Neha, whose only child is Riya. Riya's daughter-in-law Karuna is married to Tarun. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p28449/image020.png) Tarun is the brother-in-law of Robin.

questions.question: In a code language, DEVELOPERS becomes TDFDXNNDRU. How would CLASSROOMS be written?
questions.explanation: Swap adjacent letters: (1↔2), (3↔4), (5↔6), .. Then vowels (A,E,I,O,U) shift −1, consonants shift +2. Then move the last letter to the front. DEVELOPERS: Swap adjacent → EDEVOLEPSR Shift: E→D, D→F, E→D, V→X, O→N, L→N, E→D, P→R, S→U, R→T So we get = DFDXNNDRUT Last letter to front → TDFDXNNDRU CLASSROOMS: Swap adjacent → = LCSARSOOSM Shift: L→N, C→E, S→U, A→Z, R→T, S→U, O→N, O→N, S→U, M→O So we get NEUZTUNNUO Last letter to front → ONEUZTUNNU

questions.question: If 3 & 5 = 16 and 7 & 4 = 29, then what is the value of 6 & 3?
questions.explanation: Pattern: 𝑎 & b = 𝑎 𝑏 + 1 Checking with the given values: 3 & 5 = 3 5 + 1 = 15 +1 = 16 7 & 4 = 7 4 + 1 = 28 + 1 = 29 (closest rule indicating multiplication with a constant addition pattern) Applying the same rule: 6 & 3 = 6 3 + 1 = 18 + 1 = 19

questions.question: Read the following statement carefully and identify the assumption(s) that is/are implicit. Statement: A cinema hall will start a separate ticket counter for senior citizens to make the ticketing process smoother. Assumptions: I. Every senior citizen needs special assistance while purchasing tickets. II. A separate counter will completely remove waiting time for senior citizens.
options: Both Assumption I and Assumption II are implicit
Neither Assumption I nor Assumption II is implicit
questions.explanation: Statement: The cinema hall plans to start a separate ticket counter for senior citizens with the aim of making the ticketing process smoother. This indicates an intention to improve convenience or reduce difficulty, not to claim perfection or universal need. Assumption I: The statement does not assume that all senior citizens need special assistance. Creating a separate counter may be for convenience, prioritization, or comfort, even if only some senior citizens benefit. The word "every" makes the assumption too extreme and therefore not implicit. Assumption II: The statement only mentions making the process smoother, not eliminating waiting time altogether. There is no claim or hidden belief of complete removal of waiting. Hence, this assumption is not implicit. So, neither Assumption I nor Assumption II is implicit.

questions.question: If X denotes addition, Y denotes subtraction, Z denotes multiplication, and W denotes division, then find the value of the following expression: 12 (5 Z 2) X 18 Y (16 W 4) Z 3 X 7
After interchanging + and X and also interchanging 7 and 11, which of the following equations will hold true?
questions.explanation: A. (7+4) X 11=51 After interchanging: (11 X 4)+7 = 44+7 = 51 B. (11+2) X 7=77 After interchanging: (7 X 2)+11 = 14+11 = 25 C. (7+6) X 11=94 After interchanging: (11 X 6)+7 = 66+7 = 73 D. (11+5) X 7=90 After interchanging: (7 X 5)+11 = 35+11 = 46 Hence, option 'A' is correct.

questions.question: The question given below consists of a statement, followed by two arguments numbered I and II. You have to decide which of the arguments is a 'strong' argument and which is a 'weak' argument. Statement: Should financial literacy be made a compulsory subject for students of Classes 9 and 10? Argument I: Yes, it can help students understand budgeting, saving, and responsible borrowing before they start handling money independently. Argument II: No, it will reduce students' interest in sports because they will only think about money matters.
options: Both arguments I and II are strong
Neither argument I nor II is strong
questions.explanation: Argument I is directly connected to the statement and gives a practical benefit that matches the purpose of introducing the subject. Argument II makes an extreme claim without a clear link; studying financial literacy does not logically lead to losing interest in sports. So, only Argument I is strong.

questions.question: Choose the address that is exactly the same as the one given below. Room No. 3A-217, Hostel-3A, IIT Kharagpur, Kharagpur, West Bengal - 721302
options: Room No. 3A-217, Hostel 3A, IIT Kharagpur, Kharagpur, West Bengal - 721302
Room No. 3A-217, Hostel-3A, IIT Kharagpur, Kharagpur, West Bengal - 721302
Room No. 3A 217, Hostel-3A, IIT Kharagpur, Kharagpur, West Bengal - 721302
Room No. 3A-217, Hostel-3A, IIT Kharagpur, Kharagpur, West Bengal - 721320
questions.explanation: Option B matches exactly, including the hyphens in "3A-217" and "Hostel-3A". Option A removes the hyphen in "Hostel-3A". Option C removes the hyphen in "3A-217". Option D changes the PIN code to 721320.

questions.question: Find the next term: 20, 22, 26, 29, 38, ?
questions.explanation: Differences are: 22 − 20 = 2 26 − 22 = 4 29 − 26 = 3 38 − 29 = 9 These differences alternate as prime numbers and perfect squares: 2 (prime), 4 = 22, 3 (prime), 9 = 32 So the next difference should be the next prime after 3, i.e., 5. Required term = 38 + 5 = 43

questions.question: What should come in the place of the question mark (?) in the given series based on the English alphabetical order? WDJ, ?, KRK, EYV, YFL, SMU
questions.explanation: ABCDEFGHIJKLM 12345678910111213 26252423222120191817161514 ZYXWVUTSRQPON First letters: W − 6 = Q, Q − 6 = K, K − 6 = E, E − 6 = Y, Y − 6 = S Second letters: D + 7 = K, K + 7 = R, R + 7 = Y, Y + 7 = F, F + 7 = M Third letters (Reverse then +6, +7 alternately): J → Reverse(Q) +6 = W W → Reverse(D) +7 = K K → Reverse(P) +6 = V V → Reverse(E) +7 = L L → Reverse(O) +6 = U So the missing term is QKW.

questions.question: What should come in the place of the question mark (?) in the given series based on the English alphabetical order? CLRX, GMTW, KNVV, ?, SPZT, WQBS
questions.explanation: In this series, CLRX GMTW KNVV OOXU SPZT WQBS So, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: A customer purchases 3 notebooks at ₹22 each and 5 pens at ₹12 each from a stationery shop. What is the total amount the customer has to pay?
questions.explanation: Cost of 3 notebooks = 3 22 = ₹66 Cost of 5 pens = 5 12 = ₹60 Total amount to be paid = ₹66 + ₹60 = ₹126.

questions.question: There are 63 two-wheelers parked in a line near the ATM booth. Vehicle A is 18th from the left end and Vehicle B is 27th from the right end. Find the number of vehicles parked between A and B.
questions.explanation: Vehicle B is 27th from the right. Vehicle B's position from the left = 63 − 27 + 1 = 37. Vehicle A is 18th from the left. Vehicles between A and B = 37 − 18 − 1 = 18. Short trick: Number of vehicles parked between A and B = Total - (Left + Right) = 63 - (18 + 27) = 18

questions.question: In a certain code language, 'KNOW' is coded as '16-19-20-28' and 'SHED' is coded as '24-13-10-9'. How will 'FLAT' be coded in that language?

In the question, three statements are given, followed by two conclusions, I and II. You have to consider the statements to be true even if they seems to be at variance from commonly known facts. You have to decide which of the given conclusions, if any, follow(s) from the given statements. Statements: All Pitch is Field Some Pitch is Arena No Arena is Ground Conclusions: I. Some Field is Arena II. Some Pitch is not Ground
options: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows
questions.explanation: Statements: All Pitch is Field Some Pitch is Arena No Arena is Ground ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26746/image025.png) Some Field is Arena: All Pitch is Field. Some Pitch is Arena. Thus, some Field is Arena. So, conclusion I follows. Some Pitch is not Ground: Some Pitch is Arena. No Arena is Ground. Thus, the Pitch which is Arena is not Ground. So, conclusion II follows. Hence, both conclusions I and II follow.

questions.question: In each of the following questions, four words have been given out of which three are alike in some manner, while the fourth one is different. Choose out the odd one. (The words must be considered as meaningful English word and must not be related to each other based on the number of letter/consonants/vowels in the word).
questions.explanation: Young is the odd one out because except for this the rest words are Synonyms. So, option (c) is the odd one out.

questions.question: How many meaningful English words can be formed with the first, third, sixth and seventh letters of the word HORIZON (when counted from left to right) using each letter only once in each word?
questions.explanation: the first, third, sixth and seventh letters of the word HORIZON (when counted from left to right): H, R, O and N The meaningful word formed is: HORN: one of the hard pointed things that some animals have on their heads Thus, one meaningful word is formed.

questions.question: How many alphabet positions will not change if the letters in the word 'RESTORE' are arranged in reverse alphabetical order from left to right?
questions.explanation: The given word is RESTORE. If all letters are arranged in reverse alphabetical order from left to right, then we get: Original lettersRESTORE Reverse Alphabetical orderTSRROEE So, the position of 2 letters will remain not changed.

questions.question: Which of the following pairs of numbers and signs, when interchanged, will correctly solve the given mathematical equation? 32 X 96 ÷ 4 + 40 X 2 = 32
questions.explanation: Original equation: 32 X 96 ÷ 4 + 40 X 2 = 32 After interchanging numbers 32 and 96, and signs X and ÷, we get: 96 ÷ 32 X 4 + 40 ÷ 2 96 ÷ 32 = 3 3 X 4 = 12 40 ÷ 2 = 20 12 + 20 = 32 = RHS

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the fifth letter-number cluster in the same way as the second letter-number cluster is related to the first letter-number cluster and fourth letter-number cluster is related to third letter-number cluster. (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g.13- Operations on 13 such as adding /subtracting /multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed). H5 : N100 :: K9 : ? :: S12 : Y576
Under the Constitution of India, laws relating to cyberspace, such as the Information Technology Act, 2000, are enacted by Parliament because they fall under which of the following powers?
questions.explanation: Under the Constitution, legislative powers are divided in the Seventh Schedule into: Union List State List Concurrent List However, subjects that are not mentioned in any of these three lists fall under Residuary Powers, which belong to Parliament under Article 248 and Entry 97 of the Union List. Since cyber law and cyberspace issues were not foreseen when the Constitution was drafted, they are not mentioned in the three lists. Therefore, Parliament enacted the Information Technology Act, 2000 using its residuary legislative powers. Further Insights: • Article 246 distributes legislative powers between Union and States. • Article 248 gives Parliament residuary legislative powers. • Entry 97 of the Union List also covers subjects not listed elsewhere. • The Information Technology Act, 2000 is India's primary law governing cybercrime, digital signatures, and electronic commerce. • India later amended the IT Act through the Information Technology (Amendment) Act, 2008 to address modern cyber threats.

questions.question: Given below are two statements, one labeled as Assertion (A) and the other labeled as Reason (R): Assertion (A): Ajatashatru, the ruler of Magadha, took 16 years to finally defeat the powerful Vrijji confederacy of Vaishali. Reason (R): The Vrijjis were internally weakened through the diplomatic sabotage of the minister Vassakara, which broke their tribal unity.
options: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is NOT the correct explanation of (A).
questions.explanation: Both statements are historically accurate. Ajatashatru, the king of Magadha (5th century BCE), fought a prolonged war against the Vrijji (Vajji) confederacy, whose capital was Vaishali. According to Buddhist sources, this conflict lasted around 16 years. The Vrijjis were a powerful republican confederacy consisting of several clans, including the Licchavis. Because they functioned collectively and had strong internal unity, defeating them militarily was difficult. Ajatashatru's minister Vassakara used diplomatic and political strategies to weaken the confederacy. He allegedly created divisions among the tribes, weakening their unity. Once their internal cohesion broke down, Magadha was able to defeat them. Thus, the internal sabotage engineered by Vassakara explains why the war eventually ended in Magadha's victory after a long struggle. Therefore, both statements are true and the reason correctly explains the assertion. Further Insights: • The Vrijji (Vajji) confederacy was one of the Mahajanapadas of ancient India. • Vaishali, the capital of the Vrijjis, is considered one of the world's earliest republics. • Ajatashatru is also known for building the fortifications of Rajagriha and strengthening Magadha. • He reportedly used war machines such as the Mahashilakantaka and Rathamusala during his campaigns. • The victory over the Vrijjis helped Magadha emerge as the dominant power in northern India, paving the way for later empires like the Nandas and Mauryas.

questions.question: Who has recently created history by becoming the first-ever woman officer to be inducted into the Board of the Airports Authority of India (AAI)?
questions.explanation: Nivedita Dubey was appointed as Member (Human Resources) on the Board of the Airports Authority of India (AAI). With this appointment, she became the first woman officer to serve on the AAI Board, marking a historic milestone in the Indian aviation sector. She took charge on 30 January 2026, after previously serving as Regional Executive Director (Eastern Region) of AAI, where she managed multiple airports and administrative functions. Further Insights: • The Airports Authority of India (AAI) functions under the Ministry of Civil Aviation. • AAI manages over 130 airports across India. • The organization is responsible for airport infrastructure and air navigation services. • AAI was formed in 1995 through the merger of International Airports Authority of India and National Airports Authority.

questions.question: In Buddhist Stupa architecture, which specific component represents the "abode of the gods" and is designed as a square railing atop the hemispherical dome?
questions.explanation: The Harmika is a small square railing-like structure placed on top of the hemispherical dome (Anda) of a Buddhist stupa. It symbolizes the abode of the gods (devaloka) and marks the sacred space above the relic mound. From the Harmika rises the central pillar (Yasti) which supports the Chhatra (umbrella discs) representing spiritual hierarchy. Further Insights: • The Anda (dome) symbolizes the cosmic egg and the burial mound of Buddha's relics. • The Yasti (central pillar) rising from the Harmika represents the axis mundi or cosmic axis. • The Chhatra (umbrella discs) symbolize royalty, protection, and spiritual rank. • Famous stupas with elaborate Toranas include the Sanchi Stupa in Madhya Pradesh. • Pradakshina patha around the stupa is used by devotees to circumambulate in a clockwise direction.

questions.question: During the process of electrolysis, which of the following chemical reactions takes place at the cathode?
questions.explanation: In electrolysis, the cathode is the electrode where reduction occurs. Reduction means the gain of electrons by ions or atoms. During electrolysis, positively charged ions (cations) move toward the cathode and gain electrons. Example reaction: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30966/image002.png) Further Insights: • Reduction at Cathode, Oxidation at Anode. • In electrolysis, the cathode is negatively charged. • Electrolysis of water produces hydrogen gas at the cathode. • In electroplating, the metal to be deposited forms at the cathode. • Electrolysis is widely used in metal purification and electroplating industries.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding Mica: i) The Koderma - Gaya - Hazaribagh belt in Jharkhand is a major region for the production of sheet mica in India. ii) Mica is widely used in electrical and electronics industries because it has high dielectric strength and low power loss factor. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement i is correct The Koderma - Gaya - Hazaribagh belt, located mainly in Jharkhand and parts of Bihar, has historically been one of the most important mica-producing regions in India, especially known for high-quality sheet mica. Statement ii is correct Mica is highly valued in electrical and electronic industries because it has: Excellent dielectric strength (good electrical insulation) Low power loss factor High resistance to heat These properties make mica useful in capacitors, electrical equipment, and insulation materials. Further Insights: • India's major mica belts are found in Jharkhand - Bihar, Rajasthan, and Andhra Pradesh. • The Koderma region was once called the "mica capital of India." • The most common types of mica are muscovite, biotite, and phlogopite. • Mica is valued for heat resistance, elasticity, and transparency. • India was historically one of the largest exporters of sheet mica in the world.

questions.question: Which important river flows through the Siliguri Corridor (also called the "Chicken's Neck"), a narrow land passage connecting Northeast India with the rest of the country?
questions.explanation: The Teesta River flows through the Siliguri Corridor in northern West Bengal, which is a narrow stretch of land connecting mainland India to the Northeastern states. Because of its location, the river is strategically important for transport, infrastructure, water management, and security logistics in the region. Further Insights: • The Siliguri Corridor is about 20 - 22 km wide at its narrowest point. • It connects eight northeastern states with mainland India. • The corridor lies between Nepal, Bhutan, Bangladesh, and China (Tibet). • The Teesta River originates from the Tso Lhamo Lake in Sikkim.

questions.question: Which freedom fighter, known as "Gandhi Buri" (Old Lady Gandhi), was killed while leading a procession during the Quit India Movement in Tamluk, Bengal?
questions.explanation: Matangini Hazra was a freedom fighter from Bengal who actively participated in the Quit India Movement in 1942. She was popularly called "Gandhi Buri" because of her dedication to Mahatma Gandhi's ideals. In Tamluk (Midnapore district), she led a procession of protesters carrying the Indian national flag. During the protest, British police opened fire, and she was shot. Even after being hit by bullets, she continued chanting "Vande Mataram" until she died. Aruna Asaf Ali Aruna Asaf Ali is famous for hoisting the Indian National Flag at the Gowalia Tank Maidan in Mumbai during the Quit India Movement Kalpana Datta Kalpana Datta was involved in the Chittagong Armoury Raid (1930) with Surya Sen Pritilata Wadedar Pritilata Wadedar was a revolutionary associated with the Chittagong movement and died after the Pahartali European Club attack in 1932. Further Insights: • Matangini Hazra was about 73 years old when she was martyred. • The Quit India Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi on 8 August 1942. • Tamluk in Bengal later became the center of the Tamralipta National Government, a parallel government formed by freedom fighters. • Matangini Hazra was an active participant in the Civil Disobedience Movement before 1942.

questions.question: Who recently became India's youngest male analog astronaut after participating in a 2026 lunar simulation mission in Dholavira, Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Aditya Pandya, a 17-year-old space enthusiast, became India's youngest male analog astronaut after completing an eight-day lunar simulation mission in Dholavira, Kutch (Gujarat) from 1 - 8 February 2026. The mission simulated Moon-like isolation and habitat conditions on Earth. The mission was conducted by AAKA Space Studio, where a small crew lived in a container-based lunar habitat to test human survival, system reliability, and psychological endurance under simulated space conditions. Further Insights: • Analog astronaut missions simulate Moon or Mars conditions on Earth for research and training. • The 2026 mission took place in Dholavira (Rann of Kutch, Gujarat) due to its desert terrain resembling lunar landscapes. • The mission was conducted by AAKA Space Studio, a registered ISRO Space Tutor organization. • Analog missions help prepare for future human spaceflight programmes like Gaganyaan.

questions.question: During the reign of Akbar, who was commissioned to translate the Hindu epic Ramayana from Sanskrit into Persian?
questions.explanation: During Akbar's reign, a special translation bureau called Maktab Khana (House of Translation) was established at Fatehpur Sikri. The purpose was to translate important Sanskrit and regional texts into Persian, the administrative language of the Mughal Empire. Abdul Qadir Badauni was commissioned to translate the Ramayana into Persian. Although Badauni personally disagreed with Akbar's religious policies, he still carried out the translation work assigned to him. Abul Fazl Abul Fazl was Akbar's chief court historian and wrote Akbarnama and Ain-i-Akbari, but he was not responsible for translating the Ramayana. Faizi Faizi translated several Sanskrit works and was involved in the translation of Lilavati (a mathematical text), but not the Ramayana. Dara Shikoh Dara Shikoh lived later during Shah Jahan's reign and translated the Upanishads into Persian (Sirr-i-Akbar). Further Insights: • Akbar established the Maktab Khana (translation bureau) at Fatehpur Sikri in 1574. • The Mahabharata was translated into Persian as "Razmnama." • Akbar promoted the policy of Sulh-i-Kul (universal peace) to encourage religious tolerance. • Abul Fazl wrote the Akbarnama and Ain-i-Akbari, important sources for Mughal history. • Dara Shikoh later translated the Upanishads into Persian, reflecting Mughal interest in Indian philosophy.

questions.question: What was the main objective of the Suresh Tendulkar Committee, which submitted its report to the Planning Commission of India in 2009?
options: To recommend reforms in the indirect tax structure
To review the methodology for estimation of poverty
To suggest measures for doubling farmers' income
To examine the feasibility of a Universal Basic Income
questions.explanation: The Suresh Tendulkar Committee was constituted by the Planning Commission of India in 2005 under the chairmanship of economist Suresh Tendulkar. Its primary objective was to review and suggest improvements in the methodology used to estimate poverty in India. The committee submitted its report in 2009 and recommended a new method of calculating the poverty line, moving away from the older calorie-based approach. Instead, it incorporated expenditure on health, education, and other essential needs into the poverty estimation framework. Further Insights: • The Tendulkar Committee Report was submitted in 2009 to the Planning Commission. • It replaced the earlier Lakdawala Committee poverty estimation method (1993). • According to the Tendulkar methodology, 21.9% of India's population was below the poverty line in 2011 - 12. • Later, the Rangarajan Committee (2014) proposed another method for estimating poverty.

questions.question: Which former Chief of the Indian Army is the author of the controversial and currently unpublished memoir titled "Four Stars of Destiny"?
questions.explanation: The memoir "Four Stars of Destiny" was written by General Manoj Mukund Naravane, the 28th Chief of the Army Staff of India, who served from December 2019 to April 2022. The book discusses his four-decade military career, including key events such as the Galwan Valley clash with China, military leadership challenges, and defence policies. The memoir became controversial because excerpts reportedly circulated before official clearance, and the publisher clarified that the book has not yet been officially published. Further Insights: • General M. M. Naravane served as the 28th Chief of the Army Staff (2019 - 2022). • His tenure included the 2020 Galwan Valley clash between India and China. • The book reportedly discusses military strategy, leadership, and border conflicts. • The publisher Penguin Random House India holds the publishing rights to the memoir.

questions.question: What does the concave (bowed-outwards) shape of the Production Possibility Curve (PPC) indicate?
questions.explanation: A Production Possibility Curve (PPC) shows the maximum combinations of two goods that an economy can produce using its available resources. When the PPC is concave (bowed outward), it indicates increasing opportunity cost. This happens because resources are not perfectly adaptable for producing different goods. As more units of one good are produced, increasing amounts of the other good must be sacrificed, causing opportunity cost to rise. Constant Opportunity Cost Constant opportunity cost produces a straight-line PPC, not a curved one. Decreasing Opportunity Cost If opportunity cost decreased, the curve would bend inward, which is not typical in economic theory. Zero Opportunity Cost Zero opportunity cost would imply unlimited resources, which contradicts the basic economic problem of scarcity. Further Insights: • The Production Possibility Curve (PPC) represents the concept of scarcity and choice in economics. • A straight-line PPC indicates constant opportunity cost. • Points inside the PPC represent inefficient use of resources. • Points on the PPC represent efficient production levels. • Points outside the PPC are unattainable with current resources and technology.

questions.question: Which Indian shuttler won her first BWF Super 300 title at the Thailand Masters 2026 badminton tournament held in Bangkok?
questions.explanation: Indian badminton player Devika Sihag won the women's singles title at the Thailand Masters 2026, which is a BWF World Tour Super 300 event. She secured the title after Malaysia's Goh Jin Wei retired during the final, while Sihag was leading the match. This victory marked her maiden BWF Super 300 title and made her one of the few Indian women to win a World Tour title at this level. Further Insights: • The Thailand Masters is part of the BWF World Tour Super 300 circuit. • The 2026 edition was held at Nimibutr Stadium in Bangkok, Thailand. • The tournament had a total prize pool of USD 250,000. • Devika Sihag became one of the few Indian women to win a Super 300 title, joining players like Saina Nehwal and PV Sindhu. • The BWF World Tour includes categories such as Super 1000, Super 750, Super 500, and Super 300.

questions.question: Which traditional festival is celebrated by the Meitei community of Manipur to honor the "Umang Lai" (forest deities) through the ritual enactment of the creation of the universe?
questions.explanation: Lai Haraoba is an important religious and cultural festival of the Meitei people of Manipur. The term literally means "Merrymaking of the Gods." During this festival, rituals, dances, and traditional performances are conducted to honor the Umang Lai (forest deities). The ceremonies symbolically recreate the story of the creation of the universe, including the origin of life, nature, and human society. The ritual dances performed by Maibis (priestesses) depict different stages of cosmic creation and cultural traditions of the Meitei community. Sangai Festival The Sangai Festival is a modern cultural and tourism festival organized by the Government of Manipur to promote tourism, culture, and the state's heritage. Kang Rath Yatra Kang Rath Yatra in Manipur is associated with the Jagannath tradition of Hinduism, similar to the Rath Yatra celebrated in Puri. It involves pulling a chariot of Lord Jagannath and is unrelated to Umang Lai worship. Yaoshang Yaoshang is the Manipuri version of the Holi festival, celebrated with sports events, dance, and cultural activities. Further Insights: • Umang Lai refers to the sacred forest deities worshipped in Manipur. • The ritual dances of Lai Haraoba are performed mainly by priestesses called Maibis. • Ras Lila, another famous dance tradition of Manipur, is associated with Vaishnavism. • The Sangai deer, the state animal of Manipur, is found in KeibulLamjao National Park. • Yaoshang festival in Manipur lasts five days and coincides with the Hindu festival of Holi.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Union Cabinet in India: i) The term "Cabinet" was not part of the original Constitution and was inserted later through the 44th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1978 under Article 362. ii) The Cabinet is a smaller body within the Council of Ministers and meets less frequently to discuss and decide important policy matters. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement ii is Incorrect It is true that the term "Cabinet" was not present in the original Constitution, but it was introduced by the 44th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1978 under Article 352, not Article 362.Article 352 deals with National Emergency and requires that the President can proclaim an emergency only on the written advice of the Cabinet. Therefore, the statement is incorrect due to the wrong article number. Statement ii is Incorrect The Cabinet is indeed a smaller body within the Council of Ministers, but it meets more frequently, not less frequently. The Cabinet is responsible for major policy decisions and administration, so it convenes regularly. Further Insights: • Article 74 states that the President acts on the aid and advice of the Council of Ministers headed by the Prime Minister. • The Council of Ministers includes Cabinet Ministers, Ministers of State, and Deputy Ministers. • The Cabinet is the real executive authority in India's parliamentary system. • The 44th Constitutional Amendment (1978) introduced safeguards against misuse of Emergency provisions. • Article 362 earlier dealt with privileges of former princely rulers, which was later omitted by the 26th Constitutional Amendment (1971).

questions.question: The rate of heat transfer by convection between a solid surface and a surrounding fluid is governed by which of the following laws?
questions.explanation: Newton's Law of Cooling describes the rate of heat loss of a body due to convection. According to this law, the rate of heat transfer between a surface and the surrounding fluid is proportional to the temperature difference between them. Mathematically: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30966/image001.png) Where: Q = rate of heat transfer h = convective heat transfer coefficient A = surface area Tₛ = temperature of the surface T∞ = temperature of the surrounding fluid Stefan - Boltzmann Law This law describes heat transfer by radiation, not convection. It relates the energy radiated by a body to the fourth power of its temperature. Fourier's Law Fourier's Law governs heat transfer by conduction, which occurs through solids or stationary materials. Wien's Displacement Law Wien's Law relates to thermal radiation, specifically the relationship between temperature and the wavelength of maximum radiation emitted by a black body. Further Insights: • Heat transfer occurs through three modes: conduction, convection, and radiation. • Convection occurs mainly in liquids and gases due to fluid motion. • Fourier's Law describes conduction in solids. • Stefan - Boltzmann Law governs total radiation emitted by a black body. • Newton's Law of Cooling works best when the temperature difference is relatively small.

questions.question: Which Five-Year Plan of India adopted the official theme "Towards Faster and More Inclusive Growth"?
questions.explanation: The Eleventh Five-Year Plan (2007 - 2012) adopted the theme "Towards Faster and More Inclusive Growth." The main aim of this plan was to ensure that economic growth benefits all sections of society, particularly the poor, rural population, women, and marginalized communities. The plan focused on sectors such as: Education Health Agriculture Infrastructure Employment generation Further Insights: • India's First Five-Year Plan began in 1951 under the Planning Commission. • The Eleventh Plan targeted an average GDP growth rate of about 9%. • It emphasized expansion of education through schemes like Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan. • The Twelfth Five-Year Plan (2012 - 2017) was the last Five-Year Plan of India.

questions.question: Which of the following Indian sporting icons is incorrectly matched with their famous nickname?
options: Karnam Malleswari - The Iron Lady of Indian Weightlifting
Pankaj Advani - The Prince of Pune
K. D. Jadhav - The Payyoli Express
questions.explanation: The nickname "Payyoli Express" actually belongs to P. T. Usha, the legendary Indian sprinter from Payyoli, Kerala. She earned this nickname due to her exceptional speed in track events, especially in the 1980s Asian competitions and the 1984 Olympics. K. D. Jadhav was an Indian wrestler who won India's first individual Olympic medal at the 1952 Helsinki Olympics (bronze in wrestling). However, he was not known by the nickname "Payyoli Express." Further Insights: • Milkha Singh won four gold medals at the 1958 Asian Games and Commonwealth Games. • Karnam Malleswari won a bronze medal at the 2000 Sydney Olympics, India's first Olympic medal by a woman. • K. D. Jadhav won India's first individual Olympic medal in 1952. • P. T. Usha narrowly missed an Olympic medal in the 400 m hurdles at the 1984 Los Angeles Olympics. • Pankaj Advani has won multiple world titles in both billiards and snooker formats.

questions.question: Under the recently approved 'PM RAHAT' (Road Accident Victim Hospitalisation and Assured Treatment) Scheme, what is the maximum financial limit for cashless medical treatment provided to a road accident victim?
questions.explanation: Under the PM RAHAT Scheme, the Government of India provides cashless medical treatment up to ₹1.5 lakh per road accident victim. The treatment coverage is available for up to seven days from the date of the accident in designated hospitals. The scheme aims to ensure that no accident victim is denied life-saving treatment due to lack of money, especially during the "Golden Hour", when prompt medical care can save lives. Further Insights: • PM RAHAT stands for Road Accident Victim Hospitalisation and Assured Treatment. • It was launched by the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways (MoRTH) in 2026. • The scheme focuses on providing medical care during the "Golden Hour" after accidents. • It is linked with the 112 Emergency Response Support System (ERSS). • Claims are processed digitally through the eDAR (Electronic Detailed Accident Report) system and National Health Authority platforms.

questions.question: Which of the following dynasties was NOT involved in the "Tripartite Struggle" for the control of the strategic city of Kannauj in early medieval India?
questions.explanation: The Kamarupa dynasty, located in present-day Assam in Northeast India, was not involved in the Tripartite Struggle for Kannauj. Its political activities were mainly concentrated in the Brahmaputra Valley region, and it did not compete for control of Kannauj. The Tripartite Struggle (8th - 10th century CE) was a long political conflict among three major powers for dominance over Kannauj, a strategically and economically important city in northern India. The three main contenders were: The Palas of Bengal The Gurjara-Pratiharas of western and northern India The Rashtrakutas of the Deccan Further Insights: • Kannauj became politically important after the decline of Harsha's empire. • The Pala dynasty was founded by Gopala around 750 CE. • Mihira Bhoja was one of the greatest rulers of the Gurjara-Pratihara dynasty. • The Rashtrakutas ruled from Manyakheta in present-day Karnataka. • The Kamarupa kingdom was one of the earliest historical states of Northeast India, ruling the Brahmaputra Valley.

questions.question: Which chieftain of Panchalankurichi led resistance during the First Polygar War and was executed by the British at Kayathar in 1799?
questions.explanation: VeerapandiyaKattabomman was the Polygar (Palayakkarar) chieftain of Panchalankurichi in present-day Tamil Nadu. He strongly opposed the East India Company's demand for tribute (tax). His resistance led to the First Polygar War (1799) between the British and several Polygar chieftains. After being captured by the British, Kattabomman was executed at Kayathar on 16 October 1799. Further Insights: • The Polygar (Palayakkarar) system was a local administrative system in South India during the Nayak period. • The First Polygar War took place in 1799 between the British and local chieftains of Tamil Nadu. • The Second Polygar War (1800 - 1801) involved wider resistance led by the Maruthu brothers. • VeerapandiyaKattabomman became a symbol of early resistance against British colonial rule in South India.

questions.question: According to the regulations of the International Hockey Federation, what is the maximum allowed weight of a field hockey stick used in professional matches?
questions.explanation: According to the official rules of the International Hockey Federation (FIH), the maximum permissible weight of a field hockey stick is 737 grams(approx 28 oz). This rule ensures that sticks remain safe, standardized, and balanced for fair play during professional matches. Further Insights: • The International Hockey Federation (FIH) is the global governing body for field hockey. • The maximum length of a hockey stick is 105 cm. • Field hockey is played with 11 players on each side. • The Hockey World Cup is organized by the FIH every four years. • India won the Olympic gold medal in men's hockey 8 times

questions.question: Match the following historical Acts/Laws with the primary feature or personality associated with them: List I (Act/Law)List II (Key Feature/Personality) i) Special Marriage ActA) B. M. Malabari & Age raised to 12 ii) Age of Consent ActB) Fixed age at 14 for girls & 18 for boys iii) Sarda Act (1929)C) Keshab Chandra Sen & Civil Marriage Codes:
options: i - C, ii - A, iii - B
i - A, ii - B, iii - C
i - B, ii - C, iii - A
i - C, ii - B, iii - A
questions.explanation: i) Special Marriage Act → C (Keshab Chandra Sen & Civil Marriage) The Special Marriage Act of 1872, also called the Native Marriage Act (1872), was influenced by the social reformer Keshab Chandra Sen of the Brahmo Samaj. It allowed civil marriages outside traditional religious customs. ii) Age of Consent Act → A (B. M. Malabari & Age raised to 12) The Age of Consent Act, 1891 raised the minimum age of consent for girls from 10 to 12 years. Social reformer B. M. Malabari strongly campaigned for this reform to reduce child marriage and protect young girls. iii) Sarda Act (1929) → B (Fixed age at 14 for girls & 18 for boys) The Sarda Act, also known as the Child Marriage Restraint Act (1929), fixed the minimum marriage age at 14 years for girls and 18 years for boys. It was introduced by Harbilas Sarda. Further Insights: • The Sarda Act (1929) is also known as the Child Marriage Restraint Act. • The Age of Consent Act (1891) was partly triggered by the Phulmoni Dasi case. • Harbilas Sarda, a judge and legislator, introduced the Sarda Bill in the Central Legislative Assembly. • The Brahmo Samaj played a major role in promoting social reforms in marriage practices. • After independence, the Prohibition of Child Marriage Act, 2006 further strengthened laws against child marriage in India.

questions.question: What is India's rank in the Global Soft Power Index 2026, where the country recorded a score of 48.0 points?
questions.explanation: According to the Global Soft Power Index 2026 released by Brand Finance, India ranked 32nd globally with a score of 48.0 points. The ranking evaluates 193 countries based on their ability to influence others through culture, diplomacy, governance, education, and international reputation. India dropped two places compared to the previous year, reflecting weaker perceptions in areas such as governance and global reputation, though the country continues to perform well in culture, heritage, and international influence. Further Insights: • The Global Soft Power Index is published annually by Brand Finance. • The United States ranked 1st, followed by China and Japan in 2026. • The index evaluates 193 UN member states using 55 different metrics. • India performs strongly in culture & heritage, familiarity, and global influence indicators. • Weak scores in governance and reputation indicators affected India's overall ranking.

questions.question: Anurag invested ₹2700 in a bank at 18% rate of simple interest and the interest received by Anurag is ₹405. For how many months Anurag invested the amount?
questions.explanation: Let Anurag invested the amount for 'n' months. Amount invested by Anurag = ₹2700 Simple interest received by Anurag at 18% rate of interest = ₹405 2700*18*n/12*100 = 405 n = 405*2/(27*3) n = 10 Hence, Anurag invested the amount for 10 months.

questions.question: In a right-angled triangle XYZ, right angle at X, ∠ XZY = 45°. Find YZ, if XZ = 5 cm.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p21774/image011.png) ∠ XZY = 45° so ∠ XYZ = 45° Therefore XY = XZ = 5 cm By Pythagoras theorem, YZ = √ (25 + 25) = √50 = 5√2 cm

questions.question: △ABC ~ △XYZ. The areas of △ABC and △XYZ are 576 cm2 and 324 cm2, respectively, and AD and XQ are the medians of △ABC and △XYZ, respectively. If AD = 12.5 cm, then the value of XQ is:
questions.explanation: Area of ∆ABC/Area of ∆XYZ = (AD/XQ)2 √(576/324) = 12.5/XQ 24/18 = 12.5/XQ XQ = 9.375 cm

questions.question: The cost price of an article is ₹1000. Initially, the shopkeeper sells it at a profit of 10%. Later, he increases the selling price by ₹100, due to which the profit percentage becomes 20%. What is the new selling price of the article?
questions.explanation: Profit at 10% = 10% of 1000 = ₹100 Selling Price at 10% = 1000 + 100 = ₹1100 Increase in S.P = ₹100 New Selling Price = 1100 + 100 = ₹1200 Profit = 1200 − 1000 = ₹200 Profit % = 200/1000 100 = 20% New Selling Price = ₹1200

questions.question: A 250-meter long train crosses a bridge of length 500 meters in 25 seconds and crosses a platform in 30 seconds at the same speed. What is the length of the platform?
Solve the following expression 1_________(1 + sinθ) + 1_________(1 - sinθ) .
questions.explanation: "1/(1 + sinθ) + 1/(1 - sinθ) = [(1 - sinθ + 1 + sinθ) / (1 - sin2θ)] = 2/cos2 θ = 2sec2 θ "

questions.question: A right prism has a regular hexagonal base of side 5 cm and height 12 cm. Find the lateral surface area of the prism.
questions.explanation: Side of hexagon = 5 cm Height of prism = 12 cm Perimeter of the base = 6*5 = 30 cm Lateral surface area = Perimeter of the base * height = 30 * 12 = 360 cm2

questions.question: Each exterior angle of a regular polygon is 18˚. Find the number of sides of the polygon.
questions.explanation: The sum of all exterior angles in any regular polygon = 360˚ Each exterior angle = 18˚ Number of sides=(sum of all exterior angles in a regular polygon/each exterior angle) = 360/18 = 20

questions.question: Pipes A and B are inlets, and D is an outlet. A alone fills a tank in 12 hours, B alone in 18 hours, while D alone empties it in 36 hours. If all three are opened together, find the time taken to fill the tank.
questions.explanation: Net rate = 1/12 + 1/18 − 1/36 = 3/36 + 2/36 − 1/36 = 4/36 = 1/9 tank/hour Hence, the required time = 1 ÷ (1/9) = 9 hours

questions.question: Find the difference between the amounts on ₹20,000 at 10% p.a. for 1 year when interest is compounded annually versus half-yearly.
questions.explanation: Annual amount = 20 000 X 1.10 = 22 000 Half-yearly rate = 5% per half-year => amount = 20 000 X (1.05)2 = 20 000 X 1.1025 = 22 050 Difference = 22 050 − 22 000 = ₹50

questions.question: Which is a mixed recurring decimal?
questions.explanation: A mixed recurring decimal has a non-repeating part followed by a repeating part. Option A repeats "27" immediately after the decimal, so it is pure recurring. Option B repeats "09" immediately after the decimal, so it is pure recurring. Option C terminates, so it is not recurring. Option D: 0.508333... has a non-repeating part "508" and a repeating part "3".

questions.question: A perfect square between 100 and 600, whose square root is a prime number.
questions.explanation: 529 = 232, 23 is a prime number 576 = 262, 26 is not a prime number 324 = 182, 18 is not a prime number 441 = 212, 21 is not a prime number

questions.question: If x + y + z = 3, xy + yz + zx = - 4 and xyz = 6, find x3 + y3 + z3.
If a solid cone of volume 27π cm3 is kept inside a hollow cylinder whose radius and height are of that cone, then the volume of water needed to fill the empty space is:
questions.explanation: Volume of water needed to fill the empty space = volume of cylinder - volume of cone = π X r2 X h - 1/3 X π X r2 X h = 2/3 X π X r2 X h = 2 X (1/3 X π X r2 X h) = 2 X 27π cm3 = 54π cm3 Express Method When a solid cone is kept inside a hollow cylinder whose radius and height are equal to the cylinder, the ratio of the volumes of the cylinder to that of the cone = 3: 1 Given Volume of cone = 27π So, Volume of cylinder = 3 * 27π Volume of water needed to fill the empty space = Volume of cylinder - volume of cone = 81π - 27π = 54π cm3

questions.question: Convert 660° to radians.
questions.explanation: Let θ = 660° Radians = (Degrees X π) / 180 = (660 X π) / 180 = (11 X π) / 3 = 11π/3

questions.question: The angle of elevation to the top of the ladder from a point on the ground which is 10 meters away from the foot of the ladder is 60°. Find the height of the ladder.
questions.explanation: Angle of elevation = 60° ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/591696/image007.jpg) Tan 60° = h/10 √3=h/10 →h=10√3 meters

questions.question: The line 8x + 5y = 40 meets the x-axis at point M(a, b). The value of 5a + 2b is:
questions.explanation: Let M(a, b) be the x-axis intercept => b = 0. Substitute y = 0 in 8x + 5y = 40: 8a + 5 * 0 = 40 => a = 5 5a + 2b = 5 * 5 + 2 * 0 = 25.

questions.question: Bhavna's monthly salary is 30% more than Kavya's monthly salary. Leena's monthly salary is ₹1800 more than Kavya's. The sum of their yearly salaries is ₹496800. What is the sum of the monthly salaries of Bhavna and Kavya together?
If C (a, b, c) is the mid-point of A (4,7,6) and B (9,8,2), then what is the average of a, b and c?
questions.explanation: According to the mid-point theorem: - a = (4 +9)/2 =6.5 b = (7 +8)/2 = 7.5 c = (6 +2)/2 = 4 Required average = (6.5 +7.5 +4)/3 = 6

questions.question: The area of an equilateral triangle is 49√3 sq. cm. What is the circumference of a circle whose radius equals the side of the triangle? (Use π = 22/7)
questions.explanation: Area = (√3 / 4) X side² 49√3 = (√3 / 4) X side² 196√3 = √3 X side² 196 = side² side = √196 = 14 cm Radius of circle = 14 cm Circumference of circle = 2πr = 2 X (22/7) X 14 = 2 X 22 X 2 = 88 cm

questions.question: If 3cosec2θ + 7cot2θ = 13, where θ is positive acute angle, then what is the value of θ?
questions.explanation: 3cosec2θ + 7cot2θ = 13 => 3cosec2θ + 7cot2θ - 3cot2θ + 3cot2θ = 13 => 10cot2θ + 3 = 13 => cot2θ = 1 => θ = 45°

questions.question: The total surface area of a cone is 330 cm2 and its slant height is 16 cm. Find the radius of the cone.
questions.explanation: Slant height = l = 16 cm Total surface area of a cone = 330 cm2 22/7 * r * (16 + r) = 330 r2 + 16r - 105 = 0 r2 + 21r - 5r - 105 = 0 (r + 21)(r - 5) = 0 r = -21, 5 So, the radius of the cone = 5 cm

questions.question: The side of a cube is increased by 20%. By what percent does its total surface area increase?
questions.explanation: Total surface area(TSA) of cube = 6a2. New TSA = 6(1.2a)2 => 6 X 1.44a2 = 1.44 X (6a2). Percentage increase = [(1.44 − 1) X 100]% = 44%.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. India has had strong geography and historical links with her neighbours.
questions.explanation: The error is in part a) of the sentence. Use of 'geography' which is a noun needs to be replaced by 'geographical' which is an adjective to maintain a parallelism in the sentence (as 'historical' is an adjective) and to qualify the noun 'links'. Hence, the answer is option a).

questions.question: Identify the figure of speech in: "Wonderful timing!" he said, as the printer jammed during the deadline.
questions.explanation: Correct option is Irony (Option a) because the words ("wonderful timing") mean the opposite of the real situation (bad timing). Irony means saying something that clashes with the actual situation for effect. Example: "Great job!" when someone has clearly made a mistake. Option b Hyperbole is wrong because the key feature is opposite-meaning, not exaggeration. Option c Personification is wrong because no non-living thing is given a human action here. Option d Alliteration is wrong because there is no repeated starting sound pattern. Final sentence: "Wonderful timing!" he said, as the printer jammed during the deadline.

questions.question: Fill the correct degree of comparison: Among all the applicants interviewed today, Riya gave the ____ response to the case-study question.
questions.explanation: Option c is correct because "among all" shows comparison with more than two, so we need the superlative "sharpest." Rule (Degree of Comparison): Use superlative form when comparing three or more items, usually with "the/among/of all." Example: "Of all the desserts, this is the sweetest." · Option a is wrong because "sharper" is comparative (used for two). · Option b is wrong because "sharp" is the base form and does not show "most." · Option d is wrong because "more sharp" is incorrect; "sharp" takes "sharper/sharpest." Final correct sentence: Among all the applicants interviewed today, Riya gave the sharpest response to the case-study question.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined group of words. We need to hire a person who studies human societies and cultures.
questions.explanation: The underlined phrase is "person who studies human societies and cultures." a) Anthropologist - A person who studies human culture, evolution, and societies. b) Archaeologist - A person who studies ancient remains and artifacts. c) Historian - A person who studies and writes about the past. d) Sociologist - A person who studies social behavior and society. Hence, option (a) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 1.
questions.explanation: The given passage talks about "moonlighting," which means having a second job while still working at your main job. "Holding" is the best option because it means having or keeping something, like keeping or maintaining a second job while still doing your main job. Hence, (d) is the correct answer. Hammering: hitting something repeatedly with a hammer. Jeopardising: putting something at risk or in danger. Harassing: bothering or troubling someone persistently.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 2.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, "additional" fits best because it means extra or supplementary income and opportunities, which aligns with the idea of having a second job. Hence, (c) is the correct answer. Hypothetical: something that is based on a hypothesis or assumption, not necessarily real or actual. Active: something that is currently happening or in motion. Quirky: unconventional or peculiar, something that is strange or unusual.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 3.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, "productivity" fits best because it directly relates to managing one's work effectively and efficiently, which is crucial when juggling multiple jobs. Hence, (c) is the correct answer. Activity: something being done, an action or task. Probability: the likelihood of something happening, the chance or possibility. Fragility: something being delicate or easily breakable, not strong or robust.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 4.
questions.explanation: In the context of the passage discussing the potential conflicts of interest and legal or ethical issues that may arise from having multiple jobs, the best option is "avoid." This is because it's important to prevent or steer clear of these problems by understanding and following contractual obligations and company policies. Hence, (b) is the correct answer. Harness: to control or utilize something, often for a specific purpose. Entertain: to provide amusement or enjoyment to someone. Energise: to give energy or enthusiasm to someone or something.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 5.
questions.explanation: In the context of the passage discussing the challenges and importance of managing multiple roles effectively, the best option is "balance." This is because it emphasizes the need to maintain stability and well-being across both roles, ensuring that neither job overshadows the other and that both are managed effectively. Hence, (a) is the correct answer. Banter: light-hearted, playful conversation. Target: a goal or objective. Circumstance: a particular situation or condition.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can replace the underlined segment in the given sentence. The family fell asleep immediately after the dinner.
questions.explanation: Passed out-fainted. Nodded off-fell asleep. Put off-extinguished. Ran into-meet someone by chance. Among the given options, the underlined phrase in the sentence matches the meaning of the phrasal verb 'nodded off'. Hence, the correct answer is option (b). Note: Phrasal verb: A phrase that consists of a verb with a preposition or adverb or both, the meaning of which is different from the meaning of its separate parts. Example: She has always looked down on me. Here, the phrasal verb 'to look down on someone' doesn't mean that you are looking down from a higher place at someone who is below you; it means that you think that you are better than someone.

questions.question: Select the word segment that substitutes (replaces) the bracketed word segment correctly and completes the sentence meaningfully. She couldn't shake the overwhelming sense of regret as she (reflecting on the choices) that had led her down a path of missed opportunities.
questions.explanation: In this sentence, the past tense is needed to convey that the action of reflecting on the choices occurred in the past. Option (b) is the correct choice. It uses the past tense "reflected," which aligns with the context of the sentence and effectively conveys that the reflection on the choices occurred in the past. Options (a) and (c) are incorrect as 'reflect' and 'reflects' [present tense verbs] cannot be used in the sentence in the past tense. Option (d) is incorrect as 'were' [plural verb] cannot be used with 'she' [singular pronoun]. The corrected sentence: She couldn't shake the overwhelming sense of regret as she reflected on the choices that had led her down a path of missed opportunities.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Did she complete the project within the given deadline, or did unforeseen circumstances hindered her progress towards finishing it on time?
options: within the given deadline, or did unforeseen
questions.explanation: 'Do/did/does' when used as helping verbs are always followed by a main verb in its base form; the use of the past tense verb 'hindered' is incorrect, it must be replaced with the base verb 'hinder'. The correct sentence is- "Did she complete the project within the given deadline, or did unforeseen circumstances hinder her progress towards finishing it on time?" Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: Choose the option that gives the correct passive form of the sentence. The technician has been updating the phone's software for two hours.
options: The phone's software has updated by the technician for two hours.
The phone's software has been being updated by the technician for two hours.
The phone's software is being updated by the technician for two hours.
The phone's software was being updated by the technician for two hours.
questions.explanation: Option (b) is correct because the active sentence is in Present Perfect Continuous ("has been updating"). In passive, Present Perfect Continuous becomes "has/have been being + past participle". So "has been updating" changes to "has been being updated". Rule of Voice Transformation: Keep the tense same; move the object to subject position; use the correct "be" form + V3. Example: "She has been cleaning the room." →"The room has been being cleaned by her." Why other options are wrong: (a): Wrong structure and wrong verb form; "has updated" is not continuous and also missing "been/being". (c): Changes tense to present continuous ("is being updated"), not present perfect continuous. (d): Changes tense to past continuous ("was being updated"), not present perfect continuous.

questions.question: Select the correct Synonym of "IMBRUE".
questions.explanation: "Imbrue" means to stain, saturate, or soak (especially with blood). (a) Cleanse = clean (b) Purify = remove impurities (c) Saturate = completely soak (d) Disinfect = remove germs Only (c) matches the idea of soaking. Hence, option (c) is the answer.

questions.question: Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order with first part being fixed. Arrange the parts in the correct order to form a meaningful sentence. A. Going by the trend B. investing more in liquid assets C. in equities D. we should be E. than investing more
questions.explanation: DBEC is the correct order. It can be noted that the given phrase talks about some trend that gets connected through the pair DE in which the role of the trend comes into the fore and how the investment needs to be done is given in the pair BC. So, the correct order of the parts is DEBC. The complete sentence is: Going by the trend, we should be investing more in liquid assets than investing more in equities. Hence, the correct answer is option (b).

questions.question: Rearrange the following sentences to form a coherent paragraph: 1) Digital maps made travel easier, but they also changed how people pay attention to surroundings. 2) When every turn is dictated, the brain practices less "self-navigation" and remembers fewer landmarks. 3) As a result, some commuters feel disoriented on routes they have taken for months. 4) To rebuild this skill, experts suggest occasionally navigating short trips without live directions.
questions.explanation: Correct order: 1, 2, 3, 4 1 introduces the broad idea: convenience with a hidden cost. 2 explains the mechanism ("When every turn is dictated.."), so it must follow 1. 3 states the outcome ("As a result.."), so it must come after the explanation in 2. 4 suggests a remedy ("To rebuild.."), which naturally closes the paragraph. Correct option is d because it matches 1 → 2 → 3 → 4. Rule used: General statement → explanation → result → suggestion. Example: "Phones help us remember numbers, but we stop memorizing; so recall weakens; so practice helps." · a is wrong because it starts with the explanation (2) before introducing the topic (1). · b is wrong because it places the result (3) before giving the mechanism (2). · c is wrong because it starts with a result (3) without setting context first. Final correct paragraph order: 1, 2, 3, 4 Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. Appearing after the death of the originator
questions.explanation: WORDMEANING SonnetA fourteen-line poem written in iambic pentameter, employing one of several rhyme schemes, and adhering to a tightly structured thematic organization LaurelAn accolade or honour bestowed in recognition of an achievement ImpeachCharging a politician with a serious crime PosthumousAppearing after the death of the originator So, 'posthumous' is the right word here. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. In for a penny, in for a pound
options: To invest in a thing mindlessly
To intentionally invest one's time or money in a project
questions.explanation: The idiom 'in for a penny, in for a pound' is used to express someone's intention to see an undertaking through, however much time, effort, or money this entails. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym to replace the italicised word. The pilgrim asked for water for every one of us.
questions.explanation: Tourist- a person who is travelling or visiting a place for pleasure. Devotee- a strong believer in a particular religion or God. Atheist- a person who disbelieves or lacks belief in the existence of God or gods. Vagabond- a person who wanders from place to place without a home or job. The meaning of the word 'pilgrim' is 'a person who journeys to a sacred place for religious reasons'. Among the given options, the word that can replace the italicised word in the given sentence to match its meaning is option (b), 'devotee'. Hence, the correct answer is option (b).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom. She studied the reports over the weekend so she could hit the ground running when the meeting began.
options: be ready to work immediately on a new activity.
questions.explanation: The idiom "hit the ground running" means to be ready to work immediately on a new activity. Hence, option (d) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in reported speech. Rohan said to his teacher, "I'll do this project with a heart and a half."
options: Rohan told his teacher that he will do that project with a heart and a half.
Rohan told his teacher that he would do that project with a heart and a half.
Rohan told his teacher that he would do this project with a heart and a half.
Rohan told his teacher that he would be doing that project with a heart and a half.
questions.explanation: The sentence in the Direct Speech is in Future Simple Tense. Therefore, the sentence in the Indirect Speech should be in Present Conditional Tense. Future Simple (Subject + will/shall + V1 + object) should be converted into Present Conditional (Subject + would/should + V1 + object). For example: He said, "I will be in Kolkata on Friday." (Direct Speech) He said that he would be in Kolkata on Friday. (Indirect Speech) The following things in the sentence should change when changed from Direct speech to Indirect Speech: · The reporting verb 'said to' is changed into 'told'. · The pronoun 'I' is changed into 'he'. · The verb 'will do' is changed into 'would do'. · 'This project' is changed into 'that project'. Therefore, the correct answer is option (b): Rohan told his teacher that he would do that project with a heart and a half.

questions.question: Select the correctly spelt word to fill in the blank. She relaxed after a nice, long soak in the bathtub, her thoughts ________ among the stars.
questions.explanation: Correct spelling is mentioned in option (c)- 'wandering'. 'Wandering' means to walk somewhere slowly with no particular sense of direction or purpose. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. If you stopped taking drugs, you ______ so healthy and good looking.
questions.explanation: The second conditional is used to talk about ‘unreal’ or impossible things. The structure is usually if + past simple and would + base verb. It’s not important which clause comes first. Hence, option c) is the right answer.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: Among the options 'indispensible' is incorrectly spelt. The correct spelling is 'indispensable' (absolutely necessary). Multifaceted-having many different aspects or features. Explicit-stated clearly and in detail, leaving no room for confusion or doubt. Dubious-not to be relied upon; suspect Hence, the correct answer is option (b).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word. The fingerprints found at the crime scene made it evident that the suspect had been present.
questions.explanation: Evident: Clearly visible, easily understood, or obvious, requiring little or no further explanation. Permanent: Lasting or intended to last indefinitely; not temporary. Ambiguous: Open to more than one interpretation; having a double meaning. Retaliatory: Involving or characterised by retaliation; seeking revenge. Whimsical: Playfully quaint or fanciful, especially in an appealing and amusing way. Among the given options, the word 'ambiguous' is opposite in meaning to the underlined word 'evident'. So, the two words can be used as antonyms of each other. Hence, the correct answer is option (b).
